task_description: Design and implement a Python class for a simple in-memory key-value store with support for TTL (time-to-live), namespaces, and batch operations.

requirements: set(key, value, ttl_seconds=None) - Store a key-value pair with optional TTL
get(key) - Retrieve a value, returning None if expired or missing
exists(key) - Check if key exists and is not expired
set_namespace(namespace) - Set the current namespace to scope keys
batch_set(items: dict, ttl_seconds=None) - Set multiple key-value pairs at once
batch_get(keys: list) - Get multiple values at once
keys(pattern=None) - List all non-expired keys, optionally filtered by prefix pattern
clear() - Remove all keys in the current namespace
stats() - Return dict with count, namespaces, and memory estimate
constraints: Lazy expiration (check TTL on access, don't use background threads)
Keys must be strings, values can be any serializable type
Raise appropriate exceptions for invalid inputs
